---
title: "OSMnx"
---

::: {.lead}
The OSMnx tab downloads and analyzes real-world street networks from
OpenStreetMap. It exposes the full OSMnx toolbox — download, projection, routing,
accessibility, statistics, features, and elevation/terrain — adapted to the
Blender environment.
:::

The root **OSMnx** panel is a status header: once a graph is imported it shows
the active network's name, node/edge counts, place, and CRS. Most subpanels
require an imported OSMnx graph and display a hint pointing to **Download** until
one exists; subpanels appear in workflow order.

## Panels in this tab

Each panel of the addon has its own reference page:

| Panel | What it does |
| --- | --- |
| [Download](download.qmd) | Import a street network by place, point, bbox, polygon, or XML. |
| [Graph Operations](graph-operations.qmd) | Project, convert, simplify, consolidate, and truncate the graph. |
| [Network Attributes](attributes.qmd) | Add edge lengths, bearings, speeds, and travel times. |
| [Routing & Paths](routing.qmd) | Shortest path, K alternatives, and batch many-to-many routing. |
| [Accessibility](accessibility.qmd) | Isochrones, ego subgraphs, and network DBSCAN. |
| [Statistics & Centrality](statistics.qmd) | Basic stats, circuity, centrality, orientation, node-pair distances. |
| [Features & POIs](features.qmd) | Download OSM features, snap POIs, and geocoding/geometry helpers. |
| [Elevation & Terrain](elevation.qmd) | DEM sourcing, 3D positions, grades, terrain mesh, and basemaps. |
| [IO / Export](io.qmd) | GraphML round-trip, GIS export formats, and the graph cache. |

## Header status fields

When an OSMnx network is active, the header reports:

| Field | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Network** | Name of the active graph object. |
| **Nodes / Edges** | Node and edge counts. |
| **Place** | The query/place name used to download it. |
| **CRS** | The coordinate reference system (only shown once projected). |

## Typical workflow

1. **[Download](download.qmd)** a network by place, point, or bbox.
2. **Project to CRS** in **[Graph Operations](graph-operations.qmd)**, then add
   **Lengths**, **Speeds**, and **Travel Times** in
   **[Network Attributes](attributes.qmd)**.
3. Use **[Routing](routing.qmd)**, **[Accessibility](accessibility.qmd)**, and
   **[Statistics & Centrality](statistics.qmd)** for analysis, mapping results to
   attributes.
4. Add **[Elevation & Terrain](elevation.qmd)** for 3D context and a basemap,
   then visualize and render via the
   [SciGraphs Visualization panel](../scigraphs/visualization.qmd).
5. **[Export](io.qmd)** or cache the result.
